commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix championship feature data leakage in feature engineering

championship_points used cumsum() without shift, so each race row
included its own points — leaking the result into the prediction.
Now uses cumsum().shift(1).fillna(0) so each row only sees standings
from before that race. This was causing inflated pre-weekend win
probabilities (e.g. Russell 53% → 13% for 2026 R1).

domain mode (legacy). New addDeviceCard() renderer for multi-entity
devices with full-width consolidated tiles. Single-entity devices
use existing domain-specific renderers.

commit.message:
Fix India coal pricing: include statutory levies in market value

CIL base notified price was Rs 1,514/t (~$18), but buyers also pay:
royalty (14%), DMF (30% of royalty), NMET (2% of royalty), GST (5%),
and GST cess (Rs 400/t flat). All-in FSA cost ~Rs 2,270/t (~$27).
E-auction ~Rs 3,490/t (~$42). Blended estimate now ~$30/t.
India thermal market value revised from $20.4B to $30.6B.

commit.message:
Rewrite hippocampus as differentiable memory (DNC-style)

Replace hand-engineered episodic memory with fully differentiable
memory module. No discrete store/don't-store decisions, no hardcoded
thresholds — everything is soft and learned through gradient:

- Soft writes: write gate controls strength, not binary threshold
- Soft reads: content-based attention over all slots
- Learned forgetting: forget gate decides per-slot retention
- Fixed memory matrix (64 slots × 44d) on GPU, ~3KB

The system learns what to remember, retrieve, and forget entirely
through gradient descent. Bitter lesson: no hand-engineering.

Parallel IDCT on all Snapdragon cores; fix ICTDequant.hlsl for GPU path

Two-pass ICT decode (matches SwiftKVM architecture):
- Phase 1 (serial): Huffman decode → shared coefficient buffer + job list
- Phase 2 (parallel): Parallel.For dispatches IDCT across all ARM cores;
  stackalloc scratch makes InverseICT fully thread-safe
- Sequential fallback for small batches (<48 blocks, ~8 tiles)

ICTDequant.hlsl: fix X3669 dynamic resource selection error — replace
ternary texture selection with explicit if/else branches.

ARM64 native build (-p:Platform=arm64) for full Snapdragon X performance.

commit.message:
Fix E2E subprocess hang: use nil stderr instead of io.Discard

io.Discard still causes Go to create an internal pipe + copy goroutine.
When Kill() kills npx parent, child Node.js processes inherit the pipe
and keep it open, causing cmd.Wait() to hang forever. With nil stderr,
Go maps to /dev/null via OS fd duplication — no pipe, no goroutine.

commit.message:
Add glTF/GLB input support to mesh tool & pipeline (#196)

* Add glTF/GLB input support to mesh tool & pipeline

The mesh processing tool now accepts .gltf and .glb files in addition
to .obj. glTF vertices are already indexed (unlike OBJ's de-indexed
format), and tangent vectors are preserved when the glTF provides them,
skipping MikkTSpace generation.

Changes:
- tools/mesh/main.c: Add load_gltf() with ForgeGltfVertex→MeshVertex
  conversion, uint16/uint32 index handling, tangent passthrough, and
  format detection by file extension
- tools/mesh/CMakeLists.txt: Add cJSON source and include path for
  the glTF parser
- CMakeLists.txt: Guard mesh tool build with if(NOT FORGE_USE_SHIM)
  since it requires real SDL3 for file I/O
- pipeline/plugins/mesh.py: Widen extensions to [.obj, .gltf, .glb]
- tests/pipeline/test_mesh.py: Update extension assertions
- Update READMEs: add block compression to Lesson 02 "What you'll

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 5 vault-aware hooks: useFrontmatter, useTheme, usePersistentState, useNote, useDataview

New hooks in scope:
- useFrontmatter() — returns current note's YAML frontmatter, reactive to changes
- useTheme() — returns "light" or "dark", updates on Obsidian theme toggle
- usePersistentState(key, default) — like useSharedState but survives note switches
  and restarts (backed by plugin.saveData with debounced writes)
- useNote(path) — read another note: {content, frontmatter, loading, error}
- useDataview(query) — run DQL query: {values, headers, loading, error}

6 new tests (55 total), example page 12-vault-hooks with combined dashboard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove OVN from iapetus, add cross-environment DNS forwarding (#271)

* feat(cluster01): add optional development network

Add ovn-development network to base-infrastructure module following the
same pattern as gitops network. Enabled via enable_development variable.

- Add bridge and OVN network resources (conditional on enable_development)
- Add development-network profile for container attachment
- Add variables for IPv4/IPv6, NAT, and ACL configuration
- Wire up development network in cluster01 environment
- Default CIDR: 10.40.0.1/24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cluster01): add Prefect server module and environment integration

Add a new modules/prefect Terraform module for deploying Prefect workflow
orchestration server as a system container with PostgreSQL backend. Integrate
it into cluster01 as an optional service (enable_prefect = false by default),
extending the shared PostgreSQL instance with a prefect database/user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(iapetus): remove OVN networking and add cross-environment DNS forwarding

iapetus is a single host that doesn't need overlay networking. Remove all
OVN infrastructure (ovn-central, OVN networks, OVN LB) and use physical/bridge
networking with direct LAN IPs instead.

Add bidirectional DNS forwarding between iapetus and cluster01:
- iapetus forwards cluster01.accuser.dev and cluster01.incus to cluster01 CoreDNS
- cluster01 forwards accuser.dev and iapetus.incus to iapetus CoreDNS

Also fix Ansible role bugs:
- prometheus: validate prometheus_config_base64 (not prometheus_config)
- step_ca: move creates param inside command module dict

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve all medium/low severity audit findings

- Log injection (command_bus, query_bus): replace f-string embedding str(e) with
  %-style format args; exc_info=True already captures the full exception
- Truncated UUID (command_bus, query_bus): use full uuid4() string for correlation IDs
- Shared filter substring bug (handler_discovery): split module_name on '.' and check
  for exact 'shared' segment, preventing false matches on names like 'shared_commands'
- Unvalidated base_package (handler_discovery): validate all dot-separated parts are
  valid Python identifiers before attempting import
- Silent discovery failures (handler_discovery): add strict=False parameter; when
  strict=True, import and processing errors are re-raised instead of swallowed
- Handler silently skipped (handler_discovery): emit DEBUG log when a class is excluded
  due to module mismatch, making invisible skips observable
- Type annotations discarded in DI (dependency_resolver): inspect_handler_init now
  returns dict[str, type] preserving annotation per parameter; resolve_dependencies
  logs a WARNING when a provided value doesn't match the declared type

Update HandlerMetadata.dependencies from list[str] to dict[str, type] and update
all affected tests (77 passing).

commit.message:
Fix image preload: simplify state machine, fix CORS fallback

The blob preload had race conditions: when fetch() failed (CORS),
the poll only checked for blobUrl and never detected the Image
fallback — images would timeout after 15s and never show.

Simplified to a single entry object with mutation (not replacement):
- loading:true → blobUrl set (blob OK) or fallback:true (CORS)
- setImageBg polls for either state, always resolves
- Same fix applied to speaker view preload
